commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
Adds parsing for systemd Result/NRestarts/StartLimitBurst, start-limit hints in gateway status and doctor, reset-failed-before-restart behavior, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +95, Tests +241. Total +336 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main collapses failed systemd runtime to generic stopped guidance and restart omits reset-failed. I did not run a live current-main systemd repro in this read-only checkout.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blocker is a narrow predicate/test repair that automation can attempt without a product decision.

target.title: fix(gateway): surface systemd start-limit exhaustion
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

The managed gateway's systemd unit ships `Restart=always` with `StartLimitBurst=5` /
`StartLimitIntervalSec=60`. When the gateway crashes faster than that allows, systemd latches the
unit into `failed` and **stops auto-restarting it entirely**. OpenClaw collapses that terminal state
into a generic `status: "stopped"`, so `openclaw gateway status` and `openclaw doctor` only report
*"Service is loaded but not running (likely exited immediately)."* — there is no signal that systemd
has given up, and nothing ever clears the failed latch, so on systemd versions that refuse a start in
that state the gateway cannot be recovered with `openclaw gateway restart`.

## Why This Change Was Made

A crash-looped, given-up service is a distinct, actionable state (the systemd analogue of a
Kubernetes CrashLoopBackOff), not an ordinary stop. Surfacing it and making the existing restart
command recover it turns a silent dead end into a one-command fix, using state systemd already exposes.

The change is two small parts:

1. **Detect the give-up.** `readSystemdServiceRuntime` now also reads `Result`, `NRestarts`, and
   `StartLimitBurst`. Detection is `state === "failed" && (Result === "start-limit-hit" ||
   (StartLimitBurst > 0 && NRestarts >= StartLimitBurst))`. The counter path is the important one:
   once the gateway process has actually run and exited non-zero, systemd keeps `Result = exit-code`
   and never overwrites it with `start-limit-hit`, so `Result` alone misses real crash loops. While a
   unit is still inside its restart cycle its state is `activating`, not `failed`, so the check only
   fires at the terminal give-up.
2. **Recover on restart.** `openclaw gateway restart` now runs `systemctl reset-failed <unit>` before
   `restart`. It is idempotent (a no-op on a healthy unit) and clears the failed/start-limit latch so a
   crash-looped gateway comes back up.

Presentation changes are confined to the existing `stopped` branch of `openclaw gateway status` and
`openclaw doctor`; all other wording, health computation, the generated unit, and non-systemd backends
are untouched.

## User Impact

- A crash-looped gateway now reports an actionable line — *"systemd stopped restarting the gateway
  after repeated crashes; run `openclaw gateway restart` or inspect logs."* — instead of the
  misleading "exited immediately."
- `openclaw gateway restart` reliably recovers a unit that systemd has stopped restarting.
- No new config, env var, flag, or unit change. An ordinary single failure still shows the existing
  "likely exited immediately" hint.

## Evidence

- **Unit tests** (`pnpm test`): `service-runtime.test.ts` + `doctor-format.test.ts` (14), `systemd.test.ts`
  (92, incl. parse fixtures, runtime classification, and `reset-failed`-before-`restart` ordering across
  user/sudo/machine-fallback/system scopes), `status.print.test.ts` (18). `oxfmt --check` clean;
  `tsgo` core + test-types green; `pnpm build` clean.

- **Patched-OpenClaw production-path proof on real systemd 249.** The real patched functions were
  invoked (via `tsx`) against a **throwaway** systemd user unit `openclaw-gateway-slproof.service`,
  targeted through `OPENCLAW_PROFILE=slproof` (which namespaces the unit name, so the real managed
  gateway unit is never referenced or touched). It is labeled as a production-path invocation rather
  than a full `openclaw gateway restart` CLI run because the CLI restart path also waits on a gateway
  health probe that a throwaway `/bin/false`-style unit cannot satisfy. Paths below redacted (`~`).

  1. Throwaway unit driven past `StartLimitBurst`:
     ```
     $ systemctl --user show openclaw-gateway-slproof.service -p ActiveState -p SubState -p Result -p NRestarts -p StartLimitBurst
     ActiveState=failed
     SubState=failed
     Result=exit-code        # note: not start-limit-hit — systemd keeps the exec result
     NRestarts=2
     StartLimitBurst=2
     ```
  2. Patched `readSystemdServiceRuntime` reads the give-up state, and the predicate fires:
     ```
     { "status": "stopped", "state": "failed",
       "systemd": { "result": "exit-code", "nRestarts": 2, "startLimitBurst": 2 } }
     isSystemdStartLimitHit(runtime) => true
     ```
  3. Patched `openclaw doctor` render (`buildGatewayRuntimeHints`):
     ```
     systemd stopped restarting the gateway after repeated crashes.
     Recover with: openclaw --profile slproof gateway restart, then inspect logs if it keeps crashing.
     ```
  4. Patched `openclaw gateway status` render (`printDaemonStatus`):
     ```
     Runtime: stopped (state failed, sub failed, last exit 1, reason 1)
     systemd stopped restarting the gateway after repeated crashes; run openclaw gateway restart or inspect logs.
     ```
     Neither surface prints the old `Service is loaded but not running (likely exited immediately).` line.
  5. Patched restart action (`restartSystemdService`) — systemctl call ordering, captured via a PATH shim:
     ```
     systemctl --user status
     systemctl --user reset-failed openclaw-gateway-slproof.service
     systemctl --user restart openclaw-gateway-slproof.service
     ```
     `reset-failed` runs before `restart`.
  6. After the patched restart — failed latch cleared, unit recovered:
     ```
     ActiveState=active
     SubState=running
     Result=success
     NRestarts=0
     ```

  The throwaway unit was removed afterward (`stop` + `reset-failed` + unit-file delete + `daemon-reload`).

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
